title: centaurea triamularia
intro: Centaurea triamularia, also known as Red Valerian or Cornflower, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which help in treating various health issues such as insomnia, anxiety, and menstrual cramps. The medicinal properties of Centaurea triamularia are attributed to its ability to act as a sedative and a muscle relaxant, providing relief from stress and promoting better sleep. Herbal preparations of Centaurea triamularia can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as to soothe digestive issues.
